ffmpeg_encoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual ffmpeg_encoder.h(3)

NAME

ffmpeg_encoder.h - FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char *output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t **out)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const uint8_t *rgb, int pitch, uint64_t captured_ns)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const float *samples, int num_samples)
void ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

Overview

The FFmpeg encoder provides a simple interface for encoding video frames or images to disk. The encoder automatically detects the output format and codec from the file extension, handling all necessary color space conversions and encoder configuration.

Input format: RGB24 (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per pixel) Output: File on disk with automatic format/codec detection

Supported Formats & Codecs

Extension Container Video Codec Audio Codec Pixel Format Use Case .mp4, .mov MP4 H.264 AAC YUV420P General streaming, web playback .mkv Matroska H.264 AAC YUV420P High-quality archival, flexible codecs .webm WebM VP9 Opus YUV420P Web-optimized streaming, best compression .avi AVI MPEG-4 PCM YUV420P Legacy format, older systems .flv Flash H.264 AAC YUV420P Flash video, streaming history .ogv, .ogg Ogg Theora Vorbis YUV420P Free/open-source streaming .3gp 3GP H.264 AAC YUV420P Mobile devices, low bitrate .gif GIF GIF None Palette Animated clips, social media .png Image PNG None RGB24 Lossless still images, single frame .jpg, .jpeg Image MJPEG None YUVJ420P Lossy still images, single frame

Default format: MP4 (if extension not recognized) Audio: Automatically selected based on container; image formats are audio-free

Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs

Automatic Bitrate Calculation:

  • Based on resolution: ~1 Mbps per megapixel
  • Range: 500 kbps (minimum) to 5000 kbps (maximum)
  • Formula: bitrate_kbps = (width × height) / 1024, clamped to [500, 5000]
  • Provides reasonable quality for most use cases without manual tuning

Color Space Conversion:

  • Input: RGB24 (24-bit color, standard in graphics/video capture)
  • Conversion method: libswscale with SWS_BICUBIC algorithm
  • SWS_BICUBIC: Bicubic interpolation offering good quality-to-speed balance
  • Pixel format selection:
•
YUV420P: Video formats (H.264, VP9, MPEG-4) — standard ITU-R BT.601 conversion
•
YUVJ420P: JPEG-based formats (MJPEG) — uses full-range YUV (0-255)
•
RGB24: Lossless formats (PNG, GIF) — preserves exact RGB values

Quality vs. Performance:

  • H.264: Best compression, moderate speed (suitable for streaming)
  • VP9: Better quality/size ratio, slower encoding (smaller files)
  • PNG: Lossless, larger files, very slow encoding (use for critical frames)
  • GIF: Lossless animation, severely limited palette (max 256 colors)
  • MJPEG: Fast single-frame capture, reasonable compression

Single-Frame Image Handling:

  • PNG and JPEG formats (.png, .jpg, .jpeg) output only one frame
  • Multiple ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame() calls write subsequent frames to the same file
  • Useful for snapshots or periodic frame capture

Usage Examples

Encode video to MP4 (default format):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.mp4", 1920, 1080, 30, &enc);
if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    // Handle error
}
// Write frames (RGB24 format, pitch in bytes per row)
uint8_t *frame_rgb = get_frame_data();
int pitch = 1920 * 3;  // 1920 pixels × 3 bytes per pixel
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame_rgb, pitch);
// Cleanup and finalize file
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

High-quality WebM encoding (better compression than H.264):

// Simply use .webm extension
ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.webm", 1920, 1080, 24, &enc);
// ... write frames ...
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

Capture single frame to PNG (lossless snapshot):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("snapshot.png", 1920, 1080, 1, &enc);
uint8_t *frame = capture_frame();
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 1920 * 3);
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes and closes file

Animated GIF from frame sequence:

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("animation.gif", 640, 480, 10, &enc);  // 10 FPS
for (int i = 0; i < num_frames; i++) {


    uint8_t *frame = get_frame(i);


    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 640 * 3);
}
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes GIF with palette

Common Pitfalls & Best Practices

1. Pitch/Stride Parameter:

  • pitch is the byte offset from one row to the next
  • For tightly-packed RGB: pitch = width × 3
  • For aligned/padded buffers: use actual row size (may be larger)
  • Must match the actual memory layout of the input buffer

2. Memory Management:

  • Input frame data is NOT owned by encoder — caller must manage
  • Output file is owned by encoder and written/closed by ffmpeg_encoder_destroy()
  • All resources freed in ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() — always call it

3. Error Recovery:

  • Encoder is NOT usable after any error from ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame()
  • Must call ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() to clean up and finalize file
  • If file operations fail, file may be partially written

4. File Extension Sensitivity:

  • Extension matching is case-sensitive on Unix-like systems
  • Use lowercase extensions (.mp4 not .MP4) for maximum compatibility
  • Unknown extension defaults to MP4 H.264

5. Platform Support:

  • This encoder is NOT AVAILABLE on Windows (_WIN32)
  • Only POSIX systems (Linux, macOS) are supported

Performance Considerations

Encoding Speed (relative):

  • Fastest: GIF, MJPEG (lossless single-frame)
  • Fast: H.264 with default bitrate
  • Medium: VP9 (better compression at cost of speed)
  • Slow: PNG (lossless color preservation)

File Size (for 1080p @ 30fps, 10 seconds):

  • MP4 H.264: ~50-100 MB (depends on bitrate)
  • WebM VP9: ~30-60 MB (better compression)
  • AVI MPEG-4: ~100-150 MB (less efficient compression)
  • PNG: ~100+ MB per frame (lossless, single frame only)
  • GIF: ~200+ MB for 300 frames (palette limited)

Bitrate Impact on Quality:

  • Default bitrate is calculated for acceptable quality at various resolutions
  • For critical content (e.g., document sharing), consider PNG for single frames
  • For streaming, MP4 H.264 offers best balance of quality, compatibility, and size
  • VP9 recommended for highest quality requirements when file size isn't critical

Definition in file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Definition at line 163 of file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char * output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t ** out)

Create and initialize a video/image encoder.

The output format and codec are automatically determined from the file extension. See Supported Formats & Codecs for supported formats.

Bitrate: Automatically calculated from resolution (~1 Mbps per megapixel, clamped to 500-5000 kbps).

Color Space: Input is expected in RGB24 format. Internal conversion to the appropriate output pixel format (YUV420P for video, RGB24 for PNG, YUVJ420P for JPEG) is handled automatically.

Parameters

output_path Path to the output file (extension determines format and codec)
width_px Video width in pixels (must be > 0)
height_px Video height in pixels (must be > 0)
fps Frames per second (must be > 0)
out Pointer to encoder handle (set on success)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The encoder acquires resources (file handles, memory buffers) that must be released with ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(). Failure to do so may leak file handles and memory.

See also

ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame, ffmpeg_encoder_destroy, ffmpeg_encoder_formats

Definition at line 333 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.

334                                                                 {
335   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] START: output=%s, %dx%d @ %dfps", output_path, width_px, height_px, fps);
336 
337   if (!output_path || !out || width_px <= 0 || height_px <= 0)
338     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_create: invalid parameters");
339 
340   // Use default FPS if not specified (0 from config means "use default")
341   if (fps <= 0) {
342     fps = 60;
343     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: fps was 0, using default 60");
344   }
345 
346   ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*enc), ffmpeg_encoder_t *);
347   enc->width_px = width_px;
348   enc->height_px = height_px;
349 
350   // For snapshot mode, calculate output FPS based on input FPS and snapshot duration
351   // If we capture for N seconds at input_fps, we'll get ~(N * input_fps) frames
352   // Output duration should be snapshot_delay seconds
353   // So output_fps = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
354   // EXCEPT: if the input was probed, fps parameter is the actual input fps
355   // Then output_fps should equal input_fps to make frame durations sum correctly
356   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
357   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
358   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
359             snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
360 
361   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
362     // For snapshot mode: if we capture at input_fps for snapshot_delay seconds,
363     // we get approximately (input_fps * snapshot_delay) frames
364     // To make output span exactly snapshot_delay seconds:
365     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
366     // So we should use input_fps as output_fps!
367     // Example: 30fps input for 3s → ~90 frames → output at 30fps → 90/30 = 3 seconds ✓
368 
369     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
370              snapshot_delay, fps);
371     log_info("  → Will estimate ~%.0f frames, output FPS=%d makes durations sum to %.1f seconds",
372              fps * snapshot_delay, fps, snapshot_delay);
373   }
374 
375   enc->fps = fps;
376 
377   // Snapshot mode frame distribution setup
378   enc->estimated_frame_count = 0;
379   enc->estimated_frame_duration = 0;
380   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0 && fps > 0) {
381     // Estimate how many frames we'll capture
382     enc->estimated_frame_count = (int)(fps * snapshot_delay + 0.5);
383     // Each frame should have uniform duration in stream time_base (1/90000 for MP4)
384     // frame_duration = total_duration / estimated_frame_count
385     //                = snapshot_delay seconds * time_base.den / estimated_frame_count
386     // We'll set this to stream->time_base later after it's initialized
387     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode ENABLED - estimated_frame_count=%d (fps=%d * snapshot_delay=%.2f)",
388               enc->estimated_frame_count, fps, snapshot_delay);
389   } else {
390     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode disabled or invalid - snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.2f, fps=%d",
391              snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
392   }
393   enc->is_stdout_pipe = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) ? 1 : 0;
394 
395   // For codec/format detection, use output_path unless it's "-" (stdout)
396   // For stdout, use "fake.mp4" to default to MP4 format
397   const char *detection_path = output_path;
398   if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
399     detection_path = "fake.mp4"; // Fake path for format detection when piping to stdout
400     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using MP4 format for stdout piping");
401   }
402 
403   const char *codec_name = NULL, *format_name = NULL;
404   get_codec_from_extension(detection_path, &codec_name, &format_name, &enc->is_image, &enc->target_pix_fmt);
405 
406   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: %s (%s, %dx%d @ %dfps, %s)", output_path, codec_name, width_px, height_px, fps,
407             enc->is_image ? "image" : "video");
408 
409   // Allocate output media context
410   int ret = avformat_alloc_output_context2(&enc->fmt_ctx, NULL, format_name, output_path);
411   if (ret < 0) {
412     SAFE_FREE(enc);
413     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_alloc_output_context2 failed");
414   }
415 
416   // Find encoder
417   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name(codec_name);
418   if (!codec) {
419     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
420     SAFE_FREE(enc);
421     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: encoder '%s' not found", codec_name);
422   }
423 
424   // Create new stream
425   enc->stream = avformat_new_stream(enc->fmt_ctx, codec);
426   if (!enc->stream) {
427     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
428     SAFE_FREE(enc);
429     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_new_stream failed");
430   }
431 
432   // Allocate codec context
433   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
434   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
435     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
436     SAFE_FREE(enc);
437     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_alloc_context3 failed");
438   }
439 
440   // Set codec parameters
441   enc->codec_ctx->width = width_px;
442   enc->codec_ctx->height = height_px;
443   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = enc->target_pix_fmt;
444   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, fps};
445   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){fps, 1};
446 
447   // Set bitrate for high quality (music-level quality)
448   // Use ~5-10 Mbps per megapixel for visually lossless quality
449   int bitrate = (width_px * height_px * 10) / 1024; // higher quality estimate in kbps
450   if (bitrate < 4000)
451     bitrate = 4000; // Minimum 4000 kbps for quality
452   if (bitrate > 50000)
453     bitrate = 50000; // Cap at 50 Mbps max
454   enc->codec_ctx->bit_rate = bitrate * 1000;
455   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: bitrate set to %d kbps for %dx%d", bitrate, width_px, height_px);
456 
457   // Configure codec-specific options for quality
458   AVDictionary *codec_opts = NULL;
459   if (strcmp(codec_name, "libx264") == 0) {
460     // x264 fast settings for real-time rendering (optimize for speed over quality)
461     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "preset", "ultrafast", 0); // ultrafast preset for real-time encoding
462     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "crf", "28", 0); // Lower quality (crf 28 = reasonable quality, much faster)
463     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: x264 preset=ultrafast with crf=28 for real-time encoding");
464   }
465 
466   // Open codec (capture libx264 startup logs)
467   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
468     ret = avcodec_open2(enc->codec_ctx, codec, &codec_opts);
469   });
470   av_dict_free(&codec_opts);
471   if (ret < 0) {
472     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
473     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
474     SAFE_FREE(enc);
475     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_open2 failed");
476   }
477 
478   // Copy codec parameters to stream
479   ret = avcodec_parameters_from_context(enc->stream->codecpar, enc->codec_ctx);
480   if (ret < 0) {
481     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
482     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
483     SAFE_FREE(enc);
484     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_parameters_from_context failed");
485   }
486 
487   // Muxer options for proper MP4 container structure
488   AVDictionary *opts = NULL;
489 
490   // Muxer flags configuration
491   // For stdout/non-seekable output, use fragmented MP4 (frag_keyframe)
492   // For regular files, use faststart (moov at front for streaming)
493   bool is_stdout = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0);
494   if (is_stdout) {
495     // Use fragmented MP4 for stdout - starts a new fragment at each video keyframe
496     // This allows streaming without requiring file seeking (FFmpeg docs recommended)
497     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "+frag_keyframe", 0);
498     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using fragmented MP4 (+frag_keyframe) for stdout output");
499   } else {
500     // Move moov atom to the front (faststart) - helps with streaming and player compatibility
501     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "faststart", 0);
502   }
503 
504   if (enc->is_image) {
505     // Use -update flag for single frame output
506     av_dict_set(&opts, "update", "1", 0);
507   }
508 
509   // Allocate frames
510   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
511   enc->frame_encoded = av_frame_alloc();
512   enc->pkt = av_packet_alloc();
513   if (!enc->frame || !enc->frame_encoded || !enc->pkt) {
514     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
515     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
516     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
517     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
518     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
519     SAFE_FREE(enc);
520     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: frame allocation failed");
521   }
522 
523   // Set frame properties for target pixel format
524   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
525   enc->frame_encoded->width = width_px;
526   enc->frame_encoded->height = height_px;
527   ret = av_frame_get_buffer(enc->frame_encoded, 32);
528   if (ret < 0) {
529     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
530     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
531     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
532     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
533     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
534     SAFE_FREE(enc);
535     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: av_frame_get_buffer failed");
536   }
537 
538   // Create SWS context for RGB → target format conversion
539   LOG_IO("swscaler", {
540     enc->sws_ctx = sws_getContext(width_px, height_px, AV_PIX_FMT_RGB24, width_px, height_px, enc->target_pix_fmt,
541                                   SWS_BICUBIC, NULL, NULL, NULL);
542   });
543   if (!enc->sws_ctx) {
544     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
545     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
546     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
547     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
548     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
549     SAFE_FREE(enc);
550     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: sws_getContext failed");
551   }
552 
553   // Initialize audio stream if supported
554   encoder_init_audio_stream(enc, output_path);
555 
556   // Open output file or stdout
557   if (!(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE)) {
558     // For stdout output, use "pipe:1" (FFmpeg's standard for writing to stdout)
559     const char *open_path = output_path;
560     if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
561       open_path = "pipe:1"; // Use pipe:1 for stdout
562       log_debug("ffmpeg: Redirecting '-' to 'pipe:1' for stdout output");
563     }
564     ret = avio_open(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb, open_path, AVIO_FLAG_WRITE);
565     if (ret < 0) {
566       sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
567       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
568       av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
569       av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
570       avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
571       avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
572       SAFE_FREE(enc);
573       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avio_open failed for '%s'", open_path);
574     }
575   }
576 
577   // Write header (capture FFmpeg format muxer logs)
578   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
579     ret = avformat_write_header(enc->fmt_ctx, &opts);
580   });
581   av_dict_free(&opts); // Free options after use
582   if (ret < 0) {
583     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
584     sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
585     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
586     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
587     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
588     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
589     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
590     SAFE_FREE(enc);
591     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_write_header failed");
592   }
593 
594   // Now that stream->time_base is set, calculate estimated frame duration for snapshot mode
595   bool snapshot_mode_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
596   double snapshot_delay_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
597   if (snapshot_mode_after_header && enc->estimated_frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay_after_header > 0) {
598     // Each frame should span: snapshot_delay / estimated_frame_count seconds
599     // In stream time_base units: (snapshot_delay * time_base.den) / estimated_frame_count
600     enc->estimated_frame_duration =
601         (int64_t)((snapshot_delay_after_header * (double)enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->estimated_frame_count);
602     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Calculated estimated_frame_duration=%lld time_base units "
603               "for snapshot mode (snapshot_delay=%.2f, estimated_frames=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
604               (long long)enc->estimated_frame_duration, snapshot_delay_after_header, enc->estimated_frame_count,
605               enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den);
606   }
607 
608   enc->frame_count = 0;
609   enc->video_pts = 0;
610   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = 0.0;
611   enc->snapshot_elapsed_ns = 0;
612   enc->first_frame_captured_ns = 0;  // Initialize first frame timestamp
613   *out = enc;
614 
615   /* Register encoder with named registry */
616   NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER(enc, output_path, NULL);
617 
618   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] SUCCESS: encoder initialized, frame_count=0, fps=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->fps, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
619   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
620 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_image,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe, log_debug, log_info,
    LOG_IO, NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SET_ERRNO, ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_elapsed_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc)


Close and finalize the output file, releasing all encoder
    resources.




This function flushes any pending frames in the encoder, writes
    the file trailer, and releases all allocated resources (memory buffers, file
    handles, codecs).




Critical: This MUST be called to properly finalize the
    output file. Without this call, the file may be incomplete or
  unplayable.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success (currently always succeeds)


Note


Safe to call with NULL pointer (returns ASCIICHAT_OK).



After this call, enc must not be used again.




The output file is NOT guaranteed to be complete until this
    function returns.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create


Definition at line 879 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


879                                                                 {
880   if (!enc)
881     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
882 
883   NAMED_UNREGISTER(enc);
884 
885   // In snapshot mode, calculate the FPS needed to match the target duration
886   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
887   int64_t adjusted_packet_duration = 0;
888   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
889 
890   if (snapshot_mode && enc->frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay > 0) {
891     // Calculate output FPS that distributes frames across snapshot_delay seconds
892     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay
893     // packet_duration (in stream time_base) = stream_time_base.den / output_fps
894     //                                        = (stream_time_base.den * snapshot_delay) / frame_count
895     adjusted_packet_duration = (int64_t)((snapshot_delay * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
896     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - adjusting packet durations for %d frames over %.2f seconds",
897              enc->frame_count, snapshot_delay);
898     log_debug("  Adjusted packet duration: %lld units (%.6f seconds per frame)",
899               (long long)adjusted_packet_duration,
900               (double)adjusted_packet_duration / enc->stream->time_base.den);
901   }
902 
903   // Flush video encoder (capture libx264 final statistics)
904   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
905     avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, NULL);
906     int flush_pkt_count = 0;
907     while (1) {
908       int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
909       if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
910         break;
911       if (ret < 0)
912         break;
913 
914       flush_pkt_count++;
915 
916       // Set packet duration in codec time base if not already set
917       if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
918         enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
919       }
920 
921       // In snapshot mode, rescale to stream time_base and apply adjusted duration if set
922       int64_t duration_before = enc->pkt->duration;
923       av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
924       if (snapshot_mode && adjusted_packet_duration > 0) {
925         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_packet_duration;
926         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flush packet %d duration adjusted from %lld to %lld",
927                   flush_pkt_count, (long long)duration_before, (long long)adjusted_packet_duration);
928       }
929       enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
930       av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
931       av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
932     }
933   });
934 
935   // Flush audio encoder if present
936   if (enc->has_audio_stream && enc->audio_codec_ctx) {
937     // Write any remaining partial audio samples as padding
938     if (enc->audio_partial_len > 0) {
939       // Zero-pad the remaining samples
940       memset(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], 0,
941              (enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len) * sizeof(float));
942       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
943       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
944       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
945       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
946       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
947     }
948 
949     // Flush any remaining packets (capture FFmpeg audio codec logs)
950     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
951       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, NULL);
952       while (1) {
953         int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
954         if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
955           break;
956         if (ret < 0)
957           break;
958 
959         av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
960         enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
961         av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
962         av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
963       }
964     });
965   }
966 
967   // Set stream duration for proper metadata (must be before trailer)
968   if (enc->stream && enc->frame_count > 0) {
969     int64_t duration;
970     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
971     if (snapshot_mode) {
972       extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
973       double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
974       // Use actual capture elapsed time if available, otherwise use snapshot_delay
975       // This ensures frames are distributed across the correct time window
976       double actual_capture_duration = g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
977       double effective_duration = (actual_capture_duration > 0) ? actual_capture_duration : snapshot_delay;
978 
979       // In snapshot mode with wall-clock timing, the video duration should equal the actual elapsed time
980       // duration_seconds = effective_duration
981       // duration_time_base_units = effective_duration * time_base.den
982       duration = (int64_t)(effective_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den);
983 
984       // Calculate what the frame durations should be to sum to effective_duration
985       // This ensures FFmpeg plays the captured frames across the full effective_duration
986       // effective_frame_duration = effective_duration / frame_count (seconds per frame)
987       int64_t effective_frame_duration = (int64_t)((effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count) * enc->stream->time_base.den);
988 
989       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - snapshot_delay=%.2f, frames=%d, output_duration=%.2f sec",
990                snapshot_delay, enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
991       log_debug("  Using effective_duration=%.2f seconds for output", effective_duration);
992       log_debug("  stream->time_base=%d/%d, effective frame duration=%lld time_base units",
993                 enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den, (long long)effective_frame_duration);
994       log_debug("  (each frame should span %.3f seconds to total %.2f seconds)",
995                 effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
996 
997       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Setting stream->duration=%lld (effective_duration=%.3f, time_base=%d/%d, equiv=%.3f sec)",
998                (long long)duration, effective_duration,
999                enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den,
1000                (double)duration * enc->stream->time_base.num / enc->stream->time_base.den);
1001     } else {
1002       // Normal mode: calculate duration from frame count and FPS
1003       // time_base = 1 / time_base.den seconds per unit
1004       // Each frame is 1/fps seconds, so duration = frame_count / fps seconds
1005       // In time base units: duration = (frame_count / fps) * time_base.den = frame_count * time_base.den / fps
1006       duration = (int64_t)enc->frame_count * enc->stream->time_base.den / enc->fps;
1007       log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Normal mode - Set stream duration=%lld (frames=%d, fps=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
1008                 (long long)duration, enc->frame_count, enc->fps, 1, enc->stream->time_base.den);
1009     }
1010     enc->stream->duration = duration;
1011 
1012     // CRITICAL: Adjust last frame duration to ensure total duration matches stream->duration
1013     // Each frame was encoded with FPS-based duration, but snapshot mode needs them distributed
1014     // across the actual capture duration. Since we can't change past frame durations, we extend
1015     // the last frame's duration to make the sum equal the desired total.
1016     if (snapshot_mode) {
1017       // Calculate sum of all frame durations (each frame currently has the same FPS-based duration)
1018       int64_t current_total_duration = enc->video_pts;  // This is the sum of all frame durations so far
1019       int64_t desired_duration = duration;
1020 
1021       // The last frame needs to be extended to: desired - (sum of all other frames)
1022       // This ensures the video plays for exactly the snapshot_delay duration
1023       int64_t last_frame_extension = desired_duration - current_total_duration;
1024 
1025       if (last_frame_extension > 0) {
1026         // We need to extend the last frame. But we can't modify it directly after encoding.
1027         // Instead, the muxer will use stream->duration as the authoritative source.
1028         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Last frame would need extension of %lld units to reach total %lld (currently %lld)",
1029                   (long long)last_frame_extension, (long long)desired_duration, (long long)current_total_duration);
1030       }
1031     }
1032   }
1033 
1034   // Write trailer (capture FFmpeg muxer logs and final frame statistics)
1035   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1036     av_write_trailer(enc->fmt_ctx);
1037   });
1038 
1039   // Flush output buffer for stdout/pipes before closing
1040   if (enc->fmt_ctx && enc->fmt_ctx->pb) {
1041     avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1042     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flushed output buffer");
1043   }
1044 
1045   // Close output file
1046   if (enc->fmt_ctx && !(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE))
1047     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1048 
1049   // Cleanup
1050   sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
1051   av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
1052   av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
1053 
1054   // Cleanup audio resources (capture any remaining FFmpeg logs from codec cleanup)
1055   if (enc->has_audio_stream) {
1056     swr_free(&enc->audio_swr_ctx);
1057     av_frame_free(&enc->audio_frame);
1058     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1059       avcodec_free_context(&enc->audio_codec_ctx);
1060     });
1061     SAFE_FREE(enc->audio_partial_buf);
1062   }
1063 
1064   av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
1065   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1066     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
1067   });
1068   if (enc->fmt_ctx)
1069     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
1070 
1071   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: wrote %d frames", enc->frame_count);
1072   SAFE_FREE(enc);
1073   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1074 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_swr_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_debug,
    log_info, LOG_IO, NAMED_UNREGISTER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_FREE,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts.


Referenced by render_file_destroy().






void
  ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc,
  double actual_duration_sec)


Set the actual wall-clock duration for snapshot mode frame
  timing.




In snapshot mode, frames are captured over a wall-clock duration
    (e.g., 3 seconds of real time). This function should be called after all
    frames are captured to provide the actual elapsed time, allowing the encoder
    to calculate correct frame durations so the output video duration matches
    the actual capture time.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


actual_duration_sec Actual wall-clock duration in seconds (e.g., 3.5 for
  3.5 seconds)


Note


Only used in snapshot mode. In normal mode, this is
  ignored.



Should be called before ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() for the
    adjustment to take effect.




If not called, frame durations are calculated from FPS, which may
    not match actual wall-clock capture time.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 860 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


860                                                                                                     {
861   if (!enc)
862     return;
863   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = actual_duration_sec;
864 
865   // If we already have frame count, we can calculate the adjusted packet duration now
866   // This will be used for any remaining frames and during flush
867   if (enc->frame_count > 0 && actual_duration_sec > 0) {
868     // Calculate the packet duration that would make all frames fit in actual_duration_sec
869     // packet_duration_units = (actual_duration_sec * time_base.den) / frame_count
870     // This is only a starting point - the actual duration might be different if more frames are captured
871     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: %.3f sec with %d frames so far",
872               actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
873   } else {
874     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: Setting duration to %.3f seconds (already encoded %d frames)",
875             actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
876   }
877 }






References ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count, log_debug,
    log_info, and ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration.


Referenced by
  render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const float * samples, int num_samples)


Write audio samples to the output file.




Encodes and writes audio samples to the output file. Audio samples
    must be in 48kHz mono float32 format. The function internally buffers and
    accumulates samples into codec frames before encoding.




Audio Format Requirements:


    
  
  • Sample rate: 48000 Hz (48 kHz)
    • 
  
  • Channels: Mono (1 channel)
    • 
  
  • Format: 32-bit IEEE float (AV_SAMPLE_FMT_FLT)
    • 



Notes:


    
  
  • Audio is only written if the output format supports audio (MP4, WebM,
      AVI).
    • 
  
  • Image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF) ignore audio writes.
    • 
  
  • Samples are buffered internally and encoded in chunks as needed.
    • 
  
  • Timing is automatically synchronized with video using PTS (presentation
      timestamp).
    • 



Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


samples Pointer to audio samples in float32 format (not owned, used
  immediately)


num_samples Number of samples to write (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input sample data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



Safe to call on encoders without audio streams (no-op).




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame


Definition at line 793 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


793                                                                                                            {
794   if (!enc || !samples || num_samples <= 0)
795     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Silently ignore invalid calls
796 
797   // No audio stream for this format
798   if (!enc->has_audio_stream)
799     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
800 
801   // Accumulate samples into partial buffer
802   int samples_to_process = num_samples;
803   int samples_offset = 0;
804 
805   while (samples_to_process > 0) {
806     // How many samples can we add to the partial buffer?
807     int space_in_buffer = enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len;
808     int samples_to_copy = (samples_to_process < space_in_buffer) ? samples_to_process : space_in_buffer;
809 
810     // Copy samples to partial buffer
811     memcpy(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], &samples[samples_offset], samples_to_copy * sizeof(float));
812     enc->audio_partial_len += samples_to_copy;
813     samples_offset += samples_to_copy;
814     samples_to_process -= samples_to_copy;
815 
816     // If buffer is full, encode and write frame
817     if (enc->audio_partial_len >= enc->audio_frame_size) {
818       // Set up audio frame with accumulated samples
819       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
820       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
821       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
822       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
823 
824       // Send frame to encoder
825       int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
826       if (ret < 0) {
827         log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame for audio failed");
828       } else {
829         // Receive and write packets
830         while (ret >= 0) {
831           ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
832           if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
833             break;
834           if (ret < 0) {
835             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet for audio failed");
836             break;
837           }
838 
839           // Rescale timestamp and write
840           av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
841           enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
842           ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
843           av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
844           if (ret < 0) {
845             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame for audio failed");
846             break;
847           }
848         }
849       }
850 
851       // Update PTS for next frame
852       enc->audio_pts += enc->audio_frame_size;
853       enc->audio_partial_len = 0;
854     }
855   }
856 
857   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
858 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_warn_every, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const uint8_t * rgb, int pitch,
  uint64_t captured_ns)


Write a single video frame to the output file.




The frame must be in RGB24 format (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per
    pixel). Internal color space conversion is performed automatically based on
    the output format (see Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs for
    details).




Pitch Parameter: The pitch parameter specifies the byte
    offset from one row to the next. For tightly-packed RGB data: pitch =
    width_px × 3. For padded/aligned buffers: pitch must reflect the
    actual row size.




Timestamp Parameter: The captured_ns parameter is the
    wall-clock timestamp (in nanoseconds) when the frame was captured. This
    ensures video playback uses actual capture timing, not encoder processing
    time. For correct frame timing in the output video, captured_ns should be
    the time when the frame was acquired from the media source.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


rgb Pointer to frame data in RGB24 format (not copied, used immediately)


pitch Byte offset between rows (must match actual buffer layout)


captured_ns Wall-clock timestamp in nanoseconds when frame was
  captured


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input frame data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



For image-only formats (PNG, JPEG), only the last frame written is
    kept. Previous frames are discarded when the next frame is written.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 622 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


623                                                                    {
624   if (!enc || !rgb)
625     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: NULL input");
626 
627   // Initialize first frame capture time on first call
628   if (enc->first_frame_captured_ns == 0) {
629     enc->first_frame_captured_ns = captured_ns;
630     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: first frame captured at %llu ns", (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
631   }
632 
633   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_WRITE] frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
634   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d, captured_ns=%llu", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px,
635             enc->height_px, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
636 
637   // Set up RGB frame for conversion
638   uint8_t *data[1] = {(uint8_t *)rgb};
639   int linesize[1] = {pitch};
640 
641   // CRITICAL: Ensure frame format is set before each sws_scale call
642   // Some FFmpeg versions reset the format field, so we need to set it explicitly
643   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
644 
645   // Convert RGB24 to target format
646   sws_scale(enc->sws_ctx, (const uint8_t *const *)data, linesize, 0, enc->height_px, enc->frame_encoded->data,
647             enc->frame_encoded->linesize);
648 
649   // Set frame duration based on mode
650   int64_t frame_duration;
651   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
652 
653   if (enc->frame_count == 0) {
654     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame 0, snapshot_mode=%d", snapshot_mode);
655   }
656 
657   if (snapshot_mode) {
658     // Snapshot mode: use placeholder durations that will be adjusted in destroy()
659     // Each frame gets a nominal duration; destroy() will redistribute to match snapshot_delay
660     // This ensures total video duration matches snapshot_delay regardless of actual capture speed
661     frame_duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
662   } else {
663     // Normal mode: use FPS-based frame duration
664     frame_duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
665   }
666 
667   enc->frame_encoded->duration = frame_duration;
668 
669   // Track this frame's capture timestamp for duration calculation of next frame
670   if (snapshot_mode) {
671     uint64_t frame_dur_ms = (enc->previous_captured_ns > 0) ? (captured_ns - enc->previous_captured_ns) / 1000000 : 0;
672     if (enc->frame_count <= 2) {
673       log_info("ffmpeg: frame %d SET duration=%lld units (%.3fms since last, codec_base=1/%d)",
674                enc->frame_count, (long long)frame_duration, (double)frame_dur_ms,
675                enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den);
676     }
677     enc->previous_captured_ns = captured_ns;
678   }
679 
680   // Calculate PTS from actual capture timestamp
681   // time_base is {1, fps}, so 1 PTS unit = 1/fps seconds
682   // Convert elapsed nanoseconds to PTS units: elapsed_ns * fps / 1_000_000_000
683   uint64_t elapsed_ns = captured_ns - enc->first_frame_captured_ns;
684   int64_t pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)((elapsed_ns * (uint64_t)enc->fps) / 1000000000ULL);
685 
686   // In snapshot mode, distribute frames linearly across the actual duration
687   if (snapshot_mode) {
688     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
689     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
690     if (g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms > 0) {
691       // Distribute frames linearly based on their capture timestamp relative to total duration
692       // pts = (frame_elapsed_ns / total_elapsed_ns) * actual_duration_sec * fps
693       // This ensures frame 0 is at 0s and last frame is at actual_duration_sec
694       double actual_duration_sec = (double)g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
695       double total_elapsed_sec = (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
696 
697       if (total_elapsed_sec > 0.001) {
698         double frame_fraction = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
699         double target_pts_sec = frame_fraction * actual_duration_sec;
700         int64_t pts_before = pts_from_timestamp;
701         // Convert target seconds to time_base units: pts = target_sec * time_base.den / time_base.num
702         pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)(target_pts_sec * (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num);
703 
704         if (enc->frame_count < 3 || enc->frame_count % 10 == 0) {
705           log_debug("ffmpeg: snapshot frame %d: PTS distributed (before=%lld, after=%lld, sec=%lld->%lld, frac=%.3f)",
706                     enc->frame_count, (long long)pts_before, (long long)pts_from_timestamp,
707                     (long long)((double)pts_before * enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num / enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den),
708                     (long long)target_pts_sec, frame_fraction);
709         }
710       }
711     }
712   }
713 
714   // Use timestamp-based (or scaled) PTS
715   enc->frame_encoded->pts = pts_from_timestamp;
716   enc->video_pts += frame_duration;
717 
718   if (!snapshot_mode) {
719     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: PTS calculation - elapsed_ns=%llu, pts_from_timestamp=%lld, fps=%d",
720               (unsigned long long)elapsed_ns, (long long)pts_from_timestamp, enc->fps);
721   }
722 
723   // Send frame to encoder
724   int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, enc->frame_encoded);
725   if (ret < 0) {
726     log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame failed");
727     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Continue anyway
728   }
729 
730   // Receive packets from encoder and write to file
731   static int write_frame_pkt_count = 0;
732 
733   while (ret >= 0) {
734     ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
735     if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
736       break;
737     if (ret < 0) {
738       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet failed");
739       break;
740     }
741 
742     write_frame_pkt_count++;
743 
744     // Set packet duration in codec time base (duration of one frame at target FPS)
745     // This ensures proper stream duration calculation
746     if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
747       enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
748     }
749 
750     av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
751     enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
752 
753     // In snapshot mode, apply adjusted duration if snapshot_actual_duration is set
754     // This happens when we get the actual duration from the capture thread
755     bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
756     if (is_snapshot_mode && enc->snapshot_actual_duration > 0 && enc->frame_count > 0) {
757       int64_t adjusted_dur = (int64_t)((enc->snapshot_actual_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
758       // Only apply if it's significantly different from the default
759       if (adjusted_dur > enc->pkt->duration * 2) {
760         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_dur;
761         log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame packet %d duration adjusted to %lld units based on actual duration",
762                   write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration);
763       }
764     } else if (!is_snapshot_mode && write_frame_pkt_count < 3) {
765       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d, duration=%lld, snapshot_mode=%d, actual_dur=%.2f, frame_count=%d",
766                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration, is_snapshot_mode, enc->snapshot_actual_duration,
767                 enc->frame_count);
768     }
769 
770     if (write_frame_pkt_count <= 2 || write_frame_pkt_count >= 16) {
771       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d with final duration=%lld (%.6f sec in stream time_base), total frames=%d",
772                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration,
773                 (double)enc->pkt->duration / enc->stream->time_base.den, enc->frame_count);
774     }
775 
776     ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
777     av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
778     if (ret < 0) {
779       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame failed");
780       break;
781     }
782 
783     // Flush output buffer for stdout to ensure frames are written immediately
784     if (enc->is_stdout_pipe) {
785       avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
786     }
787   }
788 
789   enc->frame_count++;
790   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
791 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms,
    g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe,
    log_debug, log_info, log_warn_every,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::previous_captured_ns, SET_ERRNO,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().

    Version 0.9.4
